question: Look at the picture. The man is holding a ____ of grapes in his hands.

question: Dennis enjoys ____ in public. He is proud of his beautiful voice.
questionZh: Dennis喜歡在公眾場合___。他為自己美麗的聲音感到驕傲。

question: Mrs. Johnson can’t hear very well. If you need to talk to her, you must ____ .
questionZh: Johnson太太的聽力不太好。如果你需要和她說話，你必須___。

question: People got very excited when they watched Ms. Smith ____ at the party.
questionZh: 人們看著Smith小姐在派對上___時都非常興奮。

question: I tried on these shoes in several different ____ , and I thought the white pair looked best on me.
questionZh: 我試穿了好幾種不同___的這雙鞋，我覺得白色那雙穿起來最好看。

question: Rex did not feel the earthquake this morning. He ____ in the park at the time.
questionZh: Rex今天早上沒感覺到地震。他當時___在公園裡。

question: Mr. Lee has worked in the same store for ten years; he’s never thought about ____ his job.
questionZh: 李先生在同一家店工作了十年；他從未想過要___他的工作。

question: I didn’t take the bus today because it was ____ . All the seats were taken and a lot of students were standing.
questionZh: 我今天沒有搭公車，因為公車太___了。所有座位都坐滿了，還有很多學生站著。

question: Don’t go away when you’re cooking, ____ the food might burn.
questionZh: 烹飪時不要走開，___食物可能會燒焦。

question: Jerry wanted to know ____ he was kicked off the soccer team, but no one gave him a good reason.
questionZh: Jerry想知道他___被踢出足球隊，但沒有人給他一個好的理由。

question: Jenny is already forty, doesn’t have a job and often makes trouble for her parents. To them, she is really a(n) ____ .
questionZh: Jenny已經四十歲了，沒有工作，還經常給父母添麻煩。對他們來說，她真的是個___。

question: Students ____ to go on the school trip should ask their parents first.
questionZh: ___參加校外教學的學生應該先詢問父母。

question: Doraemon, a blue Japanese robot cat, has hated mice since his ears ____ by a mouse.
questionZh: 哆啦A夢，一隻藍色的日本機器貓，自從他的耳朵___一隻老鼠咬掉後就一直討厭老鼠。

question: If we play some interesting games in class, there ____ more fun in learning English.
questionZh: 如果我們在課堂上玩一些有趣的遊戲，學英文___更有樂趣。

question: The ____ of this shop was so bad; I never got any answer after I emailed them my questions.
questionZh: 這家店的___太差了；我發了電子郵件詢問問題後，從未得到任何回覆。

question: It’s not easy to see those islands clearly from here on sunny days, and it’s even less to see them on cloudy days.
questionZh: 在晴天從這裡要清楚地看到那些島嶼已不容易，在多雲的天氣下就更___了。

question: Do you remember the CD I was looking for for months? I ____ found it in a small shop. Look, here it is!
questionZh: 你還記得我找了好幾個月的那張CD嗎？我___在一家小店找到它了。看，就在這裡！

question: Business at Jane’s shop has not been good these days. And the new supermarket across the street only makes things ____ .
questionZh: Jane店裡的生意這幾天並不好。而對面街上新開的超市只會讓事情___。

question: Scott wasn’t sure if the young woman before him was ____ pulled him out of a car on fire.
questionZh: Scott不確定他面前的這位年輕女子是否就是___把他從著火的車裡救出來的人。

question: I ____ swimming for several years before I went to this high school. I gave it up because of heavy schoolwork.
questionZh: 在進入這所高中之前，我___游泳好幾年了。因為繁重的課業，我後來放棄了。

question: Frank Kane is so good in the movie that many people ____ he will win the best actor prize.
questionZh: Frank Kane在這部電影中表現太出色了，很多人___他將獲得最佳男主角獎。

question: The new medicine that just came out on the market ____ thousands of lives.
questionZh: 剛上市的新藥___拯救了數千條生命。

question: Now I often think of those days with Pip, my pet dog. When I read in my room, he quietly beside me.
questionZh: 現在我常常想起和我的寵物狗Pip共度的那些日子。當我在房間裡讀書時，他___靜靜地坐在我身旁。

question: Amy went to Four Seasons’ Kitchen with her mother after she collected 15 stars.  They ordered two Garden Sandwiches, an Autumn Wind, and a Winter Snow. After using the stars, how much did they pay for their meals?
questionZh: Amy在集滿15顆星後和媽媽去了Four Seasons' Kitchen。她們點了兩份Garden Sandwich、一份Autumn Wind和一份Winter Snow。使用星星後，她們付了多少錢？

question: Amy wants to bring her friends to Four Seasons’ Kitchen in August.  She looks at her calendar to pick a time to go there. Which are the time and date she  calendar 月曆 can choose?

question: According to the notes, which is the WRONG way to help a baby bird that is out of its nest?

options.A: Feed it before you take it to a hospital.
options.B: Leave it alone if it is not hurt and has feathers.
options.C: Call the animal center if you can’t find its nest.
options.D: Put it back in its nest if it is not hurt and has few feathers.

questionZh: 根據筆記，哪種方式是錯誤的幫助掉出巢的小鳥的方法？

optionsZh.B: 如果它沒有受傷且有羽毛，就讓它自己待著。
optionsZh.C: 如果找不到它的巢，就打電話給動物中心。
optionsZh.D: 如果它沒有受傷但羽毛很少，就把它放回巢中。

question: According to the notes, what do birds do if their babies have the smell of people on them?

options.A: They keep taking care of them.
options.B: They push them out of the nest.
options.C: They clean them until the smell goes away.
options.D: They leave them behind and move to a new nest.

questionZh: 根據筆記，如果嬰兒鳥身上有人的氣味，鳥類父母會怎麼做？

optionsZh.C: 清理它們直到氣味消失。

question: According to the reading, which is one of the reasons for food waste?

options.A: Stores do not know how to pack food well.
options.B: Farmers do not have enough machines to collect food.
options.C: There is no refrigerator on the truck to keep food fresh.
options.D: Factories do not have enough trucks to carry food to stores.

questionZh: 根據文章，食物浪費的原因之一是什麼？

optionsZh.A: 商店不知道如何妥善包裝食物。
optionsZh.B: 農民沒有足夠的機器來收成食物。
optionsZh.C: 卡車上沒有冰箱可以保持食物新鮮。
optionsZh.D: 工廠沒有足夠的卡車將食物運送到商店。

question: Which is true about food waste at each stage in the three parts of the world?

options.A: For each area, the highest percentage of food waste happens at Stage 5.
options.B: Europe has a lower percentage of food waste at Stage 3 than the other two areas.
options.C: North America & Oceania has a higher percentage of food waste at Stage 1 than
options.D: South & Southeast Asia has a higher percentage of food waste at Stage 4 than the

questionZh: 關於世界三個地區每個階段的食物浪費，哪一項是正確的？

optionsZh.A: 每個地區最高比例的食物浪費發生在第5階段。
optionsZh.B: 歐洲在第3階段的食物浪費比例低於其他兩個地區。
optionsZh.C: 北美和大洋洲在第1階段的食物浪費比例高於其他兩個地區。

passage:
Sometimes when the rain falls hard and fast on you, it might hurt a little.
But what happens when it hits a mosquito? A 2012 study says when a
raindrop falls on a mosquito, it’s like when a bus hits a person. Besides,
the little insect is hit by a raindrop about once every 20 seconds.
So why don’t we see many dead mosquitoes after it rains?
A mosquito is as big as a raindrop, but it is much lighter — 0.002 g only. This saves its life
in raindrop attacks. Because the mosquito is so light, when it is hit by a raindrop, it won’t
experience a force that is strong enough to kill it.
The study also found that when a mosquito is hit by a raindrop, the insect is pushed by
the raindrop and falls together with it. But the mosquito doesn’t get wet easily because it
is covered with hairs which keep off water.  After dropping about 6 cm, it will roll off the
raindrop and fly away.
However, this trick isn’t always successful.  If the mosquito flies too low when it is hit by the
raindrop, it won’t have time to fly off.  Then it will hit the ground and meet its death.
force 力量
question: What is the trick that the mosquito uses in rain?

options.A: It shakes its body fast enough to get water off.
options.B: It drops with the raindrop and then rolls off it.
options.C: It flies behind the raindrop and pushes it away.
options.D: It rides on the raindrop and lands on the ground.

questionZh: 蚊子在雨中使用的技巧是什麼？

optionsZh.A: 它快速抖動身體甩掉水分。
optionsZh.B: 它跟著雨滴落下然後滾開。
optionsZh.C: 它飛到雨滴後方把它推開。
optionsZh.D: 它騎在雨滴上然後落地。

passage:
You probably have heard Beethoven’s famous piano piece “For Elise,”
but do you know who “Elise” was?
One story is that Elise was Elisabeth Röckel. She once sang in
Beethoven’s opera Fidelio, and the two became very good friends.
Beethoven called her “Elise.”  When Röckel was leaving the city of
Vienna, Beethoven wrote “For Elise” to say goodbye to her.  Another
story is that Elise was Therese Malfatti. Beethoven was in love with
her.  He wrote a piece for her and put “For Therese” on the manuscript.  Malfatti kept the
manuscript and left it to her family when she died. Later, a man copied the manuscript
and wrote “For Elise” by mistake.  There is another possible Elise: Elise Barensfeld.  She
learned the piano from Malfatti, the same Malfatti in the second story. To help Malfatti,
Beethoven wrote the piece for her to use to teach Barensfeld.
These are just some guesses about who Elise was. We may never know the right answer.
No one knew about the piece until 1867, when the manuscript was found. This was 40 years
after Beethoven’s death, so he wasn’t around to tell us who Elise was. Only one thing is for
sure: “For Elise” will always be enjoyed. ____ opera 歌劇 manuscript 手稿
____
question: What do the three stories in the reading all talk about?
questionZh: 文章中的三個故事都在談論什麼？

optionsZh.A: 貝多芬真正的戀人是誰。
optionsZh.B: 誰把《給愛麗絲》彈得最好。
optionsZh.C: 《給愛麗絲》如何變得廣為人知。
optionsZh.D: 貝多芬為什麼寫《給愛麗絲》。

passage:
Which would you give a boy for his birthday, a toy truck or a doll? If you choose the
truck, well, you’re not alone. Most people think that some toys are for boys and some
are for girls. We might pick building toys for boys and kitchen toys for girls. But why
do we do this? Are boys and girls really born with different interests in toys?
No, they are not. Children start to show different interests in toys at age three, but
only because at this time, they begin to learn about gender and to follow what others
of their gender do. If you give a girl kitchen toys, she will think they are girls’ toys
because she sees other girls playing with them. But if you give a girl a toy car and
show her a group of girls who are playing with cars, she’ll play with the toy car and
think it is a girls’ toy. This “watch and follow” habit often becomes a rule for both
boys and girls in choosing the “right” toys.
However, this rule can be bad for children. Different toys teach children different
things. Dolls help with their early language use, and building toys are good for
learning math and science. By playing with different toys, children can find out what
they are interested in or what they are good at. If they only play one kind of toy, they
may lose this important chance. They may also get a wrong idea about what boys
or girls are able to do. Some doctors worry that children may use the same kind of
thinking when they look for jobs in the future. If they do, they may miss something
more important than just getting a chance to play with different toys. They may miss
many, perhaps better, chances in life.
gender 性別
question: What idea does Jesse Cohen talk about in the reading?

options.A: Not all kinds of toys are helpful for children’s learning.
options.B: Parents should give their children the right toys for their age.
options.C: Children should learn about their gender from playing with toys.
options.D: We should not let gender decide which toys children can play with.

questionZh: Jesse Cohen在文章中討論了什麼觀念？

optionsZh.A: 並非所有類型的玩具都對兒童學習有幫助。
optionsZh.B: 父母應該給孩子適合其年齡的玩具。
optionsZh.C: 兒童應該透過玩玩具了解自己的性別。
optionsZh.D: 我們不應該讓性別決定孩子可以玩什麼玩具。

passage:
Marie Colvin was a world-famous war reporter. She was born in New York, USA,
in 1956 and died in Homs, Syria, in 2012.
When she was a student at Yale University, Colvin decided that she wanted to be a
reporter in the future. She joined the Sunday Times, a UK newspaper, in 1985 and soon
became its Middle East reporter. Besides the Middle East, she also reported from other
parts of the world: Chechnya, Kosovo and Sri Lanka, for example.  When she was reporting
on the war in Sri Lanka, she lost her left eye. But that never stopped her. She still went to
dangerous places that few reporters wanted to visit and stayed when others left.
Colvin showed great empathy in her stories.  For her, war reporting was not just some
quick interviews and a few photos. She wrote about real people and their real lives. Her
writing made you feel so strongly about these people’s stories, just like they were your own.
She hoped that through her work the world would understand what really happened in war,
and would take action to stop the fighting.
On February 22, 2012, the day after she gave her report on the war in Homs, Colvin
was killed when the house she was staying in was attacked. She always knew what price a
war reporter might have to pay. She already lost an eye before. This time, she gave her life
to the work she could not give up — telling the true stories to the world.
war 戰爭

optionsZh.C: Marie Colvin在耶魯大學的求學歲月。

passage:
Speaking American is a great book!
Lucia Leisure ★★★★★
I just love Josh Katz’s new book! It’s about 42 in different parts of the US.
The information in the book was collected from the quiz that Katz built on the
website in 2013. The quiz asked what words people use for something, and their
answers showed interesting facts. For example, there are different names for
sports shoes. In most parts of the US, people say “tennis shoes.” In the northeast
of the country, people wear “sneakers.” In some towns, “tennis shoes” and
“sneakers” become “gym shoes.”
The use of words tells us where people are from, like Katz’s book says. But in
my experience, it also shows 43 . My family has lived in New York since my
grandparents. It’s not surprising that I say “sneakers.” But my grandfather says
“plimsolls,” and my daughter says “kicks” or “vans.” Maybe Katz can study this
difference for his next book.
website 網站

options.D: what words are often spelled differently
